title: trifolium medium subsp. eriocalycinum
intro: Trifolium medium subsp. eriocalycinum, also known as the red clover, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family and is characterized by its pink to reddish-purple flowers. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing symptoms of menopause and menopausal-related conditions such as hot flashes and night sweats. The health benefits of Trifolium medium subsp. eriocalycinum include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve blood circulation, and act as a natural anticoagulant. Herbal preparations of the plant can be consumed as tea, capsules, or supplements, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects.
